info.name: Expenses App API
info.description:
NestJS Expenses Tracker API — all routes.

Phase 8: responses standardized with `{ data, meta }` envelope on list endpoints, `{ error: { code, message, details? } }` envelope on errors, snake_case keys throughout, nested `category` on expenses, `photo_url` (signed URL, no `photo_key`) on receipts.

Phase 10.1 (v2.0): Categories are now globally seeded and read-only — only `GET /categories` exists. POST / PATCH / DELETE removed. Category rows no longer carry `user_id`.

Phase 11 (v2.0): `POST /receipts/:id/photo` now triggers an asynchronous parse — the upload returns immediately with a signed `photo_url`, and a BullMQ worker downloads the photo, calls an LLM (OpenRouter), and populates Receipt.merchant + purchased_at + Expense rows. Clients poll `GET /receipts/:id` and read `parses[0].status` (`pending` → `parsed` | `failed`). Each upload creates a new ReceiptParse row (re-uploading appends history).

item.name: Categories
item.description: Phase 10.1 (v2.0): categories are globally seeded and read-only. Only `GET /categories` exists; POST / PATCH / DELETE return 404.

response.name: 400 Validation Failed (limit out of range)

item.name: Get All Receipts (with date filter + pagination)

request.description: Returns the receipt with eagerly loaded `merchant`, `expenses[]`, and `parses[]` (newest first). v2.0 polling pattern: after `POST /receipts/:id/photo`, poll this endpoint and inspect `parses[0].status` — `pending` (worker hasn't finished), `parsed` (success — `merchant` + `purchased_at` + `expenses[]` are populated), `failed` (terminal — `error_code` set).

request.description: Uploads a receipt photo (JPEG / PNG / HEIC, magic-byte validated). v2.0 side effect: after the photo lands in S3, a `pending` ReceiptParse row is inserted and a BullMQ job is enqueued (`receipt-parse-<parse.id>`). The HTTP response returns immediately with the signed photo URL — the LLM parse runs asynchronously. Poll `GET /receipts/:id` and inspect `parses[0].status` to see the parse outcome. Re-uploading creates a NEW ReceiptParse row (each upload = distinct attempt).

response.name: 400 Invalid Photo Type (.txt)
400 Validation Failed (custom without from/to)
400 granularity must be finer than period
400 limit out of range (>100)
